items.four: Use the generated atlas.png and atlas.json in your game engine.

items.one: Use the Project panel to select folders and an atlas name.
items.two: Choose a target profile, then apply recommended settings.
items.three: Use Advanced Settings only when you need algorithm, size, cache, or watch options.

items.one: Use the Unity target profile before export.
items.two: Keep the PNG, JSON, metadata, and log files together.
items.three: Import the generated files with the Suwol Atlas Maker Unity loader.

items.one: Use the MonoGame target profile for a conservative export.
items.two: Rotation packing stays off by default for simpler draw code.
items.three: Load the JSON and PNG files with the MonoGame loader.

items.one: If export is blocked, check the short message and suggested fixes in Status.
items.two: Use View reset commands if panels or filters look wrong.
items.three: Use Help > Clear Cache only for .suwol-atlas-cache.json files.

items.one: atlas.png contains the packed texture page.
items.two: atlas.json contains sprite rectangles and pivots.
items.three: metadata.json keeps optional tags, groups, source paths, and crop data.
items.four: log.txt records packing options, warnings, and export validation details.
